title: Causal state-space tyre filter features
summary: Recent F1 modelling work shows latent tyre pace/degradation can be estimated with a Bayesian state-space model and pit stops act like state resets.
rationale: Recent F1 modelling work shows latent tyre pace/degradation can be estimated with a Bayesian state-space model and pit stops act like state resets. That is a cleaner way to represent true tyre condition than raw `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, or fixed compound life constants.
implementation_hint: Within each `Driver x Race_Year` sequence, fit an online local-level/local-trend filter on lap-time residuals and degradation proxies using only prior laps of the current stint. Export filtered state, slope, uncertainty, and shock residual as new tabular features for the existing model.
expected_effect: Improves separation between tyres that are merely old and tyres that are genuinely falling off, especially in noisy or wet conditions.
risk: State estimates can become unstable around safety cars, traffic, or outlier laps unless observation noise is robustified.
